rulings.Case Title: IRVING L. GIRSHMAN VS. THE KROGER CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Adn Motion To Compel Response To Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions [Quest]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF IRVING GIRSHMAN'S Motion To Compel Response To Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions [Quest]. Motion granted. Response to interrogatories was late. Party must provide verification. Sanctions against defendant in the amount of $690 granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA TOMCIK DR et al VS. SIDNEY FERNALD DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andm Otion Of Pltfs To Compel The Depositions Of Andrew Nere, Nora Nere, And Jan Jamison And An Extension Of The Discovery Cut-Off Date To Effectuate Said Depositions; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deponents And Their Counsel Of Record Jeffrey Lewin;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 3. PLAINTIFFS KENDRA TOMCIK, CONNIE TOMCIK'S Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Andrew Nere, Nora Nere, And Jan Jamison And An Extension Of The Discovery Cut-Off Date To Effectuate Said Depositions; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deponents And Their Counsel Of Record Jeffrey Lewin. Continue to December 29, 2010. Opposing party to provide courtesy copies of opposition to department 302 per San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. Please attach a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GENE HABEL VS. DICK GREENBURG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Gene Habel To Sign An Authorization To Obtain Confidential Records And For Sanctions For The Unreasonable Failure To Appear At Properly Noticed Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT RICHARD GREENBERG'S Motion To Compel Gene Habel To Sign An Authorization To Obtain Confidential Records And For Sanctions For The Unreasonable Failure To Appear At Properly Noticed Deposition. Granted in part. Plaintiff must sign on authorization permitting San Francisco General Hospital to release all records pertaining to the hospitalization on May 30, 2007. Records shall be marked confidential and ordered to be used only for the purposes of this litigation. Request for sanctions denied without prejudice. Failure to identify person to be sanctioned. (C.C.P. 2033.040). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PETER CRANE VS. COLBY CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Withdraw And Amend Admissions; Notice Of And Deft Clarks' Motion For An Order To Reopen Discovery Based On New Information That Pltf Needs An Additional Lumbar Surgery And To Perfect Above
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANTS COLBY CLARK, CHRISTOPHER CLARK'S Motion To Withdraw And Amend Admissions; Motion For An Order To Reopen Discovery Based On New Information That Plaintiff Needs An Additional Lumbar Surgery And To Perfect Above. Motion to withdraw and amend admissions is denied. Insufficient showing of prior counsel's mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. Prejudice to plaintiff is demonstrated. Plaintiff has prepared the case since April believing these issues are not in contention, and the trial is currently set for February after the first trial date was continued. Motion to reopen discovery for the limited issues surrounding the claim of need of a new surgical procedure related to the incident. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JP BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. LISA LEEBOVE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending Jp Builders, Inc.'S Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANTS LISA LEEBOVE, STEVE STEINBERG'S Motion For Stay Of Trial Court Proceedings Pending JP Builders, Inc.'s Appeal. Grant. Stay appropriate per C.C.P. 916 (a) and C.C.P. 128 (a)(8). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHASE BANK USA, N.A. VS. APOSTLE L. DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Notice Of Opposition To Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 6. PLAINTIFF CHASE BANK USA, N.A.'S Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Denied without prejudice. Judgment debtor fails to specify the property sought to be exempt and to provide sufficient facts to support the claim. (C.C.P. 703.520(b)).

rulings.Case Title: INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, VS. COMDISCO INVESTMENT GROUP, INC. et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 23, 2010, line 8. PLAINTIFF INFAC MANAGEMENT CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Responses To Subpoena By Comdisco. Granted.
